questions.text: Should a canonical, exhaustive list of machine-readable error codes be defined in the PRD (e.g. token_expired, token_invalid, permission_denied, user_not_found, key_not_found, validation_error), or is it acceptable to leave the specific code values as an implementation-time decision?
questions.context: A defined vocabulary prevents divergence across endpoints and ensures CLI and AF-instance clients can be written against a stable set of codes without reading implementation source.
options: Define an exhaustive list of codes in the PRD now
Define only the auth-related codes (token_expired, token_invalid, permission_denied) in the PRD; leave others to implementation
Leave all code values as implementation-time decisions

questions.text: How does the CLI handle the OAuth redirect during signup/login — local loopback redirect (e.g. http://localhost:{port}/callback), device authorization flow (code displayed in terminal, user visits URL manually), or another mechanism?
questions.context: The OAuth flow for a CLI tool differs from a browser-based flow. The mechanism chosen has security implications (e.g. loopback is acceptable per RFC 8252; device flow is better for headless environments) and must be implemented consistently.
options: Local loopback redirect (RFC 8252 native app flow)
Device authorization / code flow (user visits URL in browser, pastes code)
Both — loopback preferred, device flow as fallback for headless environments
To be decided at implementation time

questions.text: Where and in what format does the CLI store the session token after login?
questions.context: Specifying storage location and format (e.g. ~/.config/af-hub/token as a plain file, OS keychain, environment variable) is necessary for security review and for consistent behavior across platforms.
options: Plain file at a well-known path (e.g. ~/.config/af-hub/token)
To be decided at implementation time

answers.q13: Define an exhaustive list of codes in the PRD now
answers.q14: Device authorization / code flow (user visits URL in browser, pastes code)
answers.q15: Plain file at a well-known path (e.g. ~/.config/af-hub/token)
